expected_findings.description: In the busy_ratio_bounded_with_real_elapsed test, busy_now is bound from busy_before.iter() on the line above the loop, so the subtraction becomes busy_before[i].saturating_sub(busy_before[i]) — always zero. The per-worker busy duration delta metric never reports real values. The test passes trivially since 0.0 is in [0.0, 1.0].

The first ticker.tick().await in both run_stable_loop and run_unstable_loop completes immediately per Tokio's interval semantics, making the initial elapsed value very small (microseconds). This produces a misleading first busy_ratio sample that could be extremely high or erratic. Should skip the first tick or add an elapsed threshold before reporting metrics.

run_stable_loop and run_unstable_loop duplicate the ticker setup, loop structure, and elapsed calculation (~20 lines each). Could consolidate into a single loop with #[cfg(tokio_unstable)] gating only the report_unstable_metrics call, reducing duplication and ensuring loop changes only need to happen once.

false_positive_traps.description: The tokio_unstable cfg flag is set workspace-wide in .cargo/config.toml. A reviewer might flag this as risky since it affects all crates in the workspace, but the PR description explains this is required because Cargo doesn't support per-crate config within a workspace. The flag only makes additional unstable methods available — it does not change stable API behavior or runtime semantics.
